# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create Linkerd service mesh configuration for Kubernetes. Set up linkerd.io/inject annotation for automatic proxy injection. Configure server and authorization policies for services. This is for our microservices communication security.
# Category: linkerd_no_mtls

apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: my-app-namespace
  annotations:
    linkerd.io/inject: enabled
---
# Example Deployment for context (your actual application deployment)
# This deployment will automatically have the Linkerd proxy injected
# because its namespace is annotated with linkerd.io/inject: enabled.
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: my-app
  namespace: my-app-namespace
  labels:
    app: my-app
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-app
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: my-app-sa # Ensure your app has a service account
      containers:
      - name: my-app
        image: your-registry/your-app-image:latest
        ports:
        - name: http
          containerPort: 8080
---
# Example Service for context (your actual application service)
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: my-app-service
  namespace: my-app-namespace
spec:
  selector:
    app: my-app
  ports:
    - protocol: TCP
      port: 8080
      targetPort: http
---
# Define a Server policy for your application's port
# This specifies that the application on port 8080 should accept meshed traffic.
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: Server
metadata:
  name: my-app-server
  namespace: my-app-namespace
spec:
  podSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
  port: 8080 # The port on the pod that the application listens on
  # clientAuth: Required # Uncomment to enforce mTLS for this server
---
# Define a MeshTLSAuthentication policy
# This specifies which client identities are allowed to connect.
# Example: Allow only clients with the 'my-client-sa' service account
# from the 'my-client-namespace' namespace.
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta2
kind: MeshTLSAuthentication
metadata:
  name: allow-specific-client-identity
  namespace: my-app-namespace
spec:
  identities:
    - "my-client-sa.my-client-namespace.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local"
  # You can also use `serviceAccountSelector` to match multiple service accounts by labels:
  # serviceAccountSelector:
  #   matchLabels:
  #     app: my-client-app
---
# Define an AuthorizationPolicy
# This binds the Server policy to the MeshTLSAuthentication policy,
# allowing only authenticated clients as defined by `allow-specific-client-identity`
# to connect to `my-app-server`.
apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: my-app-authorization
  namespace: my-app-namespace
spec:
  targetRef:
    group: policy.linkerd.io
    kind: Server
    name: my-app-server
  requiredAuthenticationRef:
    group: policy.linkerd.io
    kind: MeshTLSAuthentication
    name: allow-specific-client-identity
  # You can also define network-based authentication, or combine them:
  # requiredAuthenticationRef:
  #   group: policy.linkerd.io
  #   kind: NetworkAuthentication
  #   name: internal-networks
  # client:
  #   meshTLS:
  #     identities:
  #       - "my-client-sa.my-client-namespace.serviceaccount.identity.linkerd.cluster.local"
